items.text: NifContext resolves a calling BEAM PID to its WorkflowHandle via the Registry.
NifContext provides synchronous access to the workflow's Recorder from a dirty NIF thread via tokio Handle::block_on.
WorkflowHandle wraps its Recorder in Arc<Mutex<Recorder>> for interior-mutable access from the NIF bridge.
engine_nifs.rs registers NIF entries for all 18 aion_flow_ffi functions.
Every NIF checks recorded history via the Resolver before executing a side effect.
run_activity/3 NIF records ActivityScheduled and ActivityStarted events before dispatching.
run_activity/3 NIF records ActivityCompleted on success or ActivityFailed on terminal failure.
run_activity/3 on replay returns the recorded result without dispatching to a worker.
The workflow_id in activity events matches the actual workflow's WorkflowId, not a random UUID.
now/0 returns the recorded_at timestamp of the last event in the workflow's history.
random/0 returns a deterministic float seeded from WorkflowId + RunId + sequence position.
random_int/2 returns a deterministic integer in the given range seeded from WorkflowId + RunId + sequence position.
now, random, and random_int produce identical values on replay as on first execution.
sleep/1 NIF records TimerScheduled, blocks until the timer fires or is cancelled, and records TimerFired.
start_timer/2 NIF records TimerScheduled and returns the timer ID.
cancel_timer/1 NIF records TimerCancelled for the named timer.
with_timeout/2 NIF wraps an operation with a deadline; records timeout expiry if the deadline fires first.
Timer NIFs on replay return recorded results without scheduling real timers.
receive_signal/2 NIF blocks until a signal arrives and records SignalReceived.
send_signal/3 NIF delivers a signal to a target workflow and records SignalSent.
Signal NIFs on replay return recorded results without delivering or waiting for real signals.
register_query/3 NIF registers a query handler for the workflow.
reply_query/2 NIF sends a query response.
dispatch_query/2 NIF dispatches a query to a workflow and returns the response.
spawn_child/3 NIF starts a child workflow and records ChildWorkflowStarted.
await_child/1 NIF blocks until the child completes and records ChildWorkflowCompleted or ChildWorkflowFailed.
Child NIFs on replay return recorded results without spawning real child processes.
collect_all/2 NIF dispatches multiple activities and waits for all results.
collect_race/2 NIF dispatches multiple activities and returns the first result, cancelling the rest.
collect_map/2 NIF dispatches activities from a mapped collection.
Concurrency NIFs record individual activity events for each dispatched activity.
The engine monitors each workflow process via beamr process monitoring.
Normal process exit records WorkflowCompleted with the return value as the result payload.
Abnormal process exit records WorkflowFailed with the exit reason as the error payload.
After terminal event recording, the workflow handle's cached status is updated in the Registry.
Zombie workflows (Running status with dead process) do not occur after this cluster lands.
